'''Stuart Arthur Spruce''' (born 3 January 1971) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1980s, 1990s and 2000s. He played as a {{rlp|fb}} for the [[Widnes Vikings]] and the [[Bradford Bulls]], and represented [[England national rugby league team|England]] and [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] at international level.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Playing career==

===Widnes===
Stuart Spruce made his début for [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] during the 1989–90 season, under coach [[Doug Laughton]]. When regular fullback [[Alan Tait]] quit for [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]], Spruce became the permanent fullback. [[Bradford Bulls]] paid a record £120,000 for a fullback when they signed Spruce in May 1996. The previous record of £90,000 was paid by [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] to [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] for David Lyon in September 1992.<ref>Rothmans RL Yearbook 1997 by Raymond Fletcher and David Howes page 379 {{ISBN|0747277648}}</ref>

===Bradford Bulls===
Spruce played for [[Bradford Bulls]] at {{rlp|fb}} in the [[1999 Super League Grand Final]] which was lost to [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]]. He became known as a good defensive player and an excellent offense. [[James Lowes]], who played with him throughout his time with Bradford, paid this tribute: "Stuart has been one of the best fullbacks of his generation. He is a great talker on the field and a devastating runner."

He played for Bradford until the week before the 2001 Rugby League [[Challenge Cup]] Final, when he announced his retirement due to injury.<ref>{{cite web |title=What a send off! |url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/news/8040134.what-a-send-off/ |website=Bradford Telegraph and Argus |access-date=7 April 2023 |date=7 July 2001}}</ref>

===Return to Widnes===
Spruce came out of retirement to play for Widnes during their inaugural [[Super League]] season in 2002.<ref>{{cite web |title=Spruce makes Widnes return |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/1530839.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=7 April 2023 |date=7 September 2001}}</ref> He made a total of 54 appearances before retiring permanently at the end of the [[Super League VIII|2003 Super League season]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Widnes veteran Spruce announces retirement |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/rugby-league/widnes-veteran-spruce-announces-retirement-72696.html |website=The Independent |access-date=7 April 2023 |date=9 January 2004}}</ref>

===International honours===
Spruce won [[Cap (sport)|cap]]s for [[England national rugby league team|England]] while at Widnes in 1992 against Wales and while at Bradford Bulls in 2000 against Russia (sub) and Fiji, and won [[Cap (sport)|cap]]s for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] while at Widnes in 1993 against France, and while at Bradford Bulls in 1996 against Papua New Guinea, Fiji, and New Zealand (3 matches).

==Coaching==
Stuart served briefly as caretaker head coach after Neil Kelly left.
